rulings.Case Title: ROBERT G BAKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS RELFECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion In Support Of Defendant Campbell Soup Companys Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set Three; Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 1. Defendant Campbell Soup Company¿s motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories is taken off calendar as moot because the court will be issuing an order granting defendant¿s motion for summary judgment which was taken under submission on August 18. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is denied. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES CARMICHAEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (BP)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 2. Defendant Marcal Associates, Ltd.'s motion to compel FURTHER RESPONSE TO REQUEST, DEMAND INSPECTION, PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL R BARNES VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs To Further Respond To Requests For Admission, Special Interrogatories And Form Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 3. Defendant Novelis Corporation.¿s motion to compel further discovery responses is continued to September 28 to give the parties an opportunity to meet and confer regarding the asserted inadequacy of plaintiffs¿ supplemental and amended responses. If the parties have not resolved all issues before then, by 4pm on September 27 the parties must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org stating, without argument, the unresolved issues and then appear at the hearing on September 28 at 9am. Defendant¿s request for sanctions is granted. Sanctions are awarded in favor of defendant in the amount of $1840, calculated at 8 hours at $225 per hour plus $40 in costs. Plaintiffs¿ initial responses lacked substantial justification. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTHA JOAN HILL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Pathology Materials For Testing
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Rockwell Automation, Inc.¿s motion to compel pathology testing is granted in part. Defendant may conduct testing provided that it complies with all of the conditions stated in Dr. Hammar¿s declaration. Dr. Hammar¿s declaration is the only medical evidence before the court and thus there is no basis for the court to do anything other than follow his suggested conditions = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MARTHA JOAN HILL et al VS. AC AND S, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Joinder In Defendant Rockwell Automation Inc.'S Motion To Compel Pathology Materials For Destructive Testing
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Discovery Calendar for Wednesday, September 15, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:00 a.m., Line 4. Defendant Gardner Denver, Inc.¿s joinder is taken off calendar in light of the ruling on Rockwell Automation¿s motion. = (220/HEK)
